verses.1: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God,
verses.2: just as it is written in Isaiah the prophet: “BEHOLD, I AM SENDING MY MESSENGER BEFORE YOU, WHO WILL PREPARE YOUR WAY;
verses.3: THE VOICE OF ONE CALLING OUT IN THE WILDERNESS, ‘PREPARE THE WAY OF THE LORD, MAKE HIS PATHS STRAIGHT!’”
verses.4: John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
verses.5: And all the country of Judea was going out to him, and all the people of Jerusalem; and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.
verses.6: John was clothed with camel’s hair and wore a leather belt around his waist, and his diet was locusts and wild honey.
verses.7: And he was preaching, saying, “After me One is coming who is mightier than I, and I am not fit to bend down and untie the straps of His sandals.
verses.8: I baptized you with water; but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
verses.9: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
verses.10: And immediately coming up out of the water, He saw the heavens opening, and the Spirit, like a dove, descending upon Him;
verses.11: and a voice came from the heavens: “You are My beloved Son; in You I am well pleased.”
verses.12: And immediately the Spirit brought Him out into the wilderness.
verses.13: And He was in the wilderness for forty days, being tempted by Satan; and He was with the wild animals, and the angels were serving Him.
verses.14: Now after John was taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of God,
verses.15: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe in the gospel.”
verses.16: As He was going along the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and Andrew, the brother of Simon, casting a net in the sea; for they were fishermen.
verses.17: And Jesus said to them, “Follow Me, and I will have you become fishers of people.”
verses.18: Immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
verses.19: And going on a little farther, He saw James the son of Zebedee, and his brother John, who were also in the boat mending the nets.
verses.20: Immediately He called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men, and went away to follow Him.
verses.21: They went into Capernaum; and immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and began to teach.
verses.22: And they were amazed at His teaching; for He was teaching them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
verses.23: Just then there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
verses.24: saying, “What business do you have with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are: the Holy One of God!”
verses.25: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be quiet, and come out of him!”
verses.26: After throwing him into convulsions and crying out with a loud voice, the unclean spirit came out of him.
verses.27: And they were all amazed, so they debated among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him.”
verses.28: Immediately the news about Him spread everywhere into all the surrounding region of Galilee.
verses.29: And immediately after they left the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
verses.30: Now Simon’s mother-in-law was lying sick with a fever; and they immediately spoke to Jesus about her.
verses.31: And He came to her and raised her up, taking her by the hand, and the fever left her, and she served them.
verses.32: Now when evening came, after the sun had set, they began bringing to Him all who were ill and those who were demon-possessed.
verses.33: And the whole city had gathered at the door.
verses.34: And He healed many who were ill with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and He would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew who He was.
verses.35: And in the early morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house, and went away to a secluded place, and prayed there for a time.
verses.36: Simon and his companions eagerly searched for Him;
verses.37: and they found Him and said to Him, “Everyone is looking for You.”
verses.38: He said to them, “Let’s go somewhere else to the towns nearby, so that I may also preach there; for this is why I came.”
verses.39: And He went into their synagogues preaching throughout Galilee, and casting out the demons.
verses.40: And a man with leprosy came to Jesus, imploring Him and kneeling down, and saying to Him, “If You are willing, You can make me clean.”
verses.41: Moved with compassion, Jesus reached out with His hand and touched him, and said to him, “I am willing; be cleansed.”
verses.42: And immediately the leprosy left him, and he was cleansed.
verses.43: And He sternly warned him and immediately sent him away,
verses.44: and He said to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
verses.45: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely and to spread the news around, to such an extent that Jesus could no longer publicly enter a city, but stayed out in unpopulated areas; and they were coming to Him from everywhere.
